title: fix(playground): reset TTS voice when switching to a model without it
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Playground voice state leaked across TTS providers: the model-change effect only reset `selectedVoice` when the new model had voices, so ElevenLabs → Qwen3-TTS (which has no `voices` in the registry → zero voices) kept a stale voice (e.g. `rachel`), which the API rejects as "voice not available for this model".
- The effect now clears `selectedVoice` to `""` when the new model has no voices, and still resets to the first valid voice when it has some.

Risks: UI-only React state change in `PlayGenerator.tsx`; no API/backend/registry changes. Biome clean; no new tsc errors in the edited region.

Fixes #10473

gist.headline: Clearing the throat
gist.blurb: Fixed a quirky bug in the playground where switching text-to-speech models would sometimes keep the old voice selected. Now, when you switch to a model without specific voices, we properly clear the slate.
gist.summary: Fixed a React state leak in the playground where switching from a TTS model with voices (like ElevenLabs) to one without (like Qwen3-TTS) retained the previous voice selection, causing API rejections.
gist.impact: Users will no longer encounter 'voice not available' errors when switching between text-to-speech models in the playground.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing tiny studio headphones, working at a retro audio mixing board, clearing out old cassette tapes (representing the stale voice state).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny studio headphones, adjusting sliders on a retro audio mixing board. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head helps by clearing away old cassette tapes. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic.
